name: McCoy Farm near Adna
short_description: Private rockhounding site near Ceres offering agate, jasper, and petrified wood specimens in the gentle rolling terrain of Lewis County's volcanic geology.
description:
McCoy Farm near Adna is a private rockhounding location situated in the lower Cascades region of Washington, where volcanic activity and sedimentary deposits have created abundant opportunities for finding agate, jasper, and petrified wood. The site sits on gently sloping terrain at 618 feet elevation, making it accessible to rockhounders of various skill levels despite its private land status. The rolling landscape typical of this area reflects the underlying geology shaped by Eocene to Miocene volcanic episodes that created silica-rich mineral deposits throughout western Washington.

This location is prized by collectors seeking quality agate specimens and colorful jasper nodules weathered from ancient volcanic deposits. The gentle slope and relatively accessible terrain make it suitable for families and beginning rockhounders, though access is dependent on permission from the property owner. Petrified wood specimens are occasionally found in association with the agate and jasper deposits, adding variety to what collectors can recover.

The Lewis County area has a long history of small-scale rockhounding and mineral collection, with many private farms and properties allowing limited collecting by permission. McCoy Farm represents the kind of private site that rewards those willing to ask permission and establish relationships with landowners. Always bring proper tools for surface collecting and be prepared to leave the site in good condition to maintain access for future rockhounders.
history: This area sits within the Cascade Range foothills where Eocene to Miocene volcanic activity deposited silica-rich materials that altered and crystallized into agate and jasper nodules. The overlying sedimentary layers and weathered volcanic soils have exposed these minerals across the gentle slopes of Lewis County for millennia, making private farms like McCoy Farm natural collecting sites.
tips: Search along the exposed hillsides and washed-out areas where rain and erosion have naturally exposed agate and jasper nodules. Focus on areas with loose soil and eroded banks where specimens are more easily visible and accessible. Bring a small pry bar or chisel to carefully extract specimens from clay and soil matrices without damaging them. Look for the characteristic waxy luster and color banding of agate - specimens here often display whites, grays, and browns typical of Cascade Range volcanic deposits.
terrain_notes: Gently sloping terrain at 618 feet elevation typical of the lower Cascades foothills. Expect mixed vegetation including oak, fir, and brush. Walking distances from parking to productive collecting areas typically range from short walks to moderate hikes across soft earth and exposed banks. Footing is generally stable but can be muddy after rain due to clay-rich volcanic soils.
written_directions: From Ceres, head east on State Route 6 for approximately 8 miles toward Adna. Look for local farm roads branching north or south depending on specific site access. Contact property owner for precise directions and permission before visiting. GPS coordinates provided (46.617128, -123.141353) serve as approximate location reference.
road_conditions: Local farm roads may be unpaved or gravel. Standard passenger vehicles acceptable during dry conditions. May require high clearance or awd during wet weather.
parking_notes: Parking typically available in farm yard area or designated spots near collecting areas. Permission from property owner required before parking or collecting.
collection_rules: Private property - permission from landowner required before collecting. Collect responsibly and leave the property in good condition to preserve access for future rockhounders. Pack out all trash. Do not trespass or collect without explicit permission.
rules:
Permission from property owner required
Personal use collection only
Respect property boundaries
Pack out all trash
Leave site in good condition
nearest_services: Ceres, approximately 8 miles west. Chehalis, approximately 15 miles south, offers full services including gas, food, and water.
accessibility_notes: Gently sloping terrain accessible to most hikers and families. Soft volcanic soil and clay-rich ground may be muddy after rain. No wheelchair access on unpaved farm roads and collecting areas. Sturdy hiking boots or work shoes recommended for muddy conditions.

faq.question: What gemstones can you find at McCoy Farm near Ceres, Washington?
faq.answer: McCoy Farm is known for agate, jasper, petrified wood, and quartz specimens. Collectors often find banded agate nodules in whites, grays, and browns, along with colorful jasper pieces weathered from ancient Cascade Range volcanic deposits.

faq.question: Do I need permission to rockhound at McCoy Farm near Adna?
faq.answer: Yes. McCoy Farm is private property and requires explicit permission from the landowner before you visit or collect. Always contact the property owner first and respect their boundaries and rules regarding collection limits and site access.

faq.question: What is the best time of year to rockhound at McCoy Farm in Washington?
faq.answer: The best season is April through October when weather is relatively dry and the terrain is most accessible. Summer months (June - August) offer the most favorable conditions, though spring and fall also provide good collecting opportunities.

faq.question: How difficult is rockhounding at McCoy Farm for beginners?
faq.answer: McCoy Farm is beginner-friendly with easy, gently sloping terrain at just 618 feet elevation. Children ages 6 and up can participate in surface collecting. The soft volcanic soil and exposed banks make it relatively easy to locate and extract specimens.

faq.question: What tools do I need to collect agate and jasper at McCoy Farm?
faq.answer: A small pry bar or chisel is useful for carefully extracting agate and jasper nodules from clay matrices. Bring a small bucket or bag for specimens, work gloves for protection, and sturdy boots since the ground can be muddy. A magnifying glass helps identify quality specimens in the field.

faq.question: How far is McCoy Farm from Ceres and what are the nearest services?
faq.answer: McCoy Farm is approximately 8 miles east of Ceres via State Route 6. Ceres offers basic services, while Chehalis (15 miles south) provides full amenities including gas stations, restaurants, and supplies. Bring water and supplies before heading to the remote farm location.
